speaker: MATT
raw_text: Thank you guys for your amazing support of this initiative. It's been awesome to watch all the Critters come together to help support our troops there. It's been awesome. We also have a bunch of new live content coming to our Critical Role Twitch stream in the next few weeks, including the season finale of All Work No Play.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes! Operation Supply Drop is my charity that we are supporting this November in honor of Veterans Day. OSD is an amazing charity. They've helped over 750,000 members of the military community. In active service, in their transition back to civilian life, they help the families of the people that have served. These guys are so great. They have skill fairs, they help people with job training, resumes, anything that'll take them from being a military back into the real world. To me, it's so personal. My family has been in the military for, oh god, decades. So if you can, share with your friends and family, give a little love. Everything you need to know about the charity, or just donate, is at critrole.com/osd.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Thank you guys for your amazing support of this initiative. It's been awesome to watch all the Critters come together to help support our troops there. It's been awesome. We also have a bunch of new live content coming to our Critical Role Twitch stream in the next few weeks, including the season finale of All Work No Play.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes! Operation Supply Drop is my charity that we are supporting this November in honor of Veterans Day. OSD is an amazing charity. They've helped over 750,000 members of the military community. In active service, in their transition back to civilian life, they help the families of the people that have served. These guys are so great. They have skill fairs, they help people with job training, resumes, anything that'll take them from being a military back into the real world. To me, it's so personal. My family has been in the military for, oh god, decades. So if you can, share with your friends and family, give a little love. Everything you need to know about the charity, or just donate, is at critrole.com/osd.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I'll tell you over drinks. This is not the best place to have these discussions, eh? Perhaps after a few rounds. Come! To The Bloated Cup!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's what I was going to ask  if you were a fan or not.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "He doesn't bother me, he leaves us well enough alone, we follow the rules, we maintain all of the benefits of being under the banner, and I'm left to my own devices."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Any foes of yours in this Darktow? Anyone we should look out for?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There are a number of unsavory types, but I've made a bit of a reputation of being a little-- eh? People don't like to tangle with that too much, eh?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, why would you get wrapped up in that? Yeah, let's go grab a drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hmm.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How long have you been a pirate?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Let's see. Open on the seas? About 12, 13 years."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whoa!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How did you fall into it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I got sick of following somebody else's orders."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where do you hail from?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Aye aye. Captain." Looks to Avantika. Avantika right now is deep in the cups. At this point, a figure turns around the corner. You see a male gnome: short, maybe in his 40s or so with spiked hair. It's thinning on the top, but you can see he's taken thick balms of some kind to try and make it look intensely young, and failing halfway. Big mutton chops that come down to a very pointed chin, and he's dressed in fairly decent clothing, a nice silk vest with billowing sleeves out of his shirt, with some bangles that clink around his wrists. He comes and finds you on the opposite side and looks across to Avantika grinning. He goes, "Well well well, it's been a while since we've seen you 'round here!" She turns to him and goes, "Ah, Sorris! You're back from that fool's errand, I see. How was the Shattered Teeth?" He goes, "Eh, foggy and cold as shit. How goes your cloud-chasing journeys, love?" She goes, "Grand! We might have scored a fine shipwreck, and a promise of salvage, eh?" Looks back to him. "You up for hire, perhaps?" He goes, "Eh, tempting but, nah. I'm still fresh off the water, want to enjoy myself a while." She's like, "All right, very well, we'll be in port for a day or two while we get some repairs done. If you change your mind, well, just start shouting. I'm sure we'll find your annoying voice easy enough." He goes, "(giggles) Sure that is, eh? You're all new 'round here."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'll take a drink and I'll plop it down right in front of her and I'll take a knee on the floor, so I'm kneeling next to her on the other side of Avantika. I'll say: Hey listen, no hard feelings, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There is no hard feelings. I understand the reasoning, and the captain's word is law. I am excited to see the great things you accomplish as the quartermaster of our ship."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right, whatever's best for the ship.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Precisely, and for the captain."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, I feel the presence of Uk'otoa smiling on all of us and I know that our partnership here will be a beneficial one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can only agree."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, this has been fun. If you need anything, as your quartermaster, you just let me know.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Aye aye. Captain." Looks to Avantika. Avantika right now is deep in the cups. At this point, a figure turns around the corner. You see a male gnome: short, maybe in his 40s or so with spiked hair. It's thinning on the top, but you can see he's taken thick balms of some kind to try and make it look intensely young, and failing halfway. Big mutton chops that come down to a very pointed chin, and he's dressed in fairly decent clothing, a nice silk vest with billowing sleeves out of his shirt, with some bangles that clink around his wrists. He comes and finds you on the opposite side and looks across to Avantika grinning. He goes, "Well well well, it's been a while since we've seen you 'round here!" She turns to him and goes, "Ah, Sorris! You're back from that fool's errand, I see. How was the Shattered Teeth?" He goes, "Eh, foggy and cold as shit. How goes your cloud-chasing journeys, love?" She goes, "Grand! We might have scored a fine shipwreck, and a promise of salvage, eh?" Looks back to him. "You up for hire, perhaps?" He goes, "Eh, tempting but, nah. I'm still fresh off the water, want to enjoy myself a while." She's like, "All right, very well, we'll be in port for a day or two while we get some repairs done. If you change your mind, well, just start shouting. I'm sure we'll find your annoying voice easy enough." He goes, "(giggles) Sure that is, eh? You're all new 'round here."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'll take a drink and I'll plop it down right in front of her and I'll take a knee on the floor, so I'm kneeling next to her on the other side of Avantika. I'll say: Hey listen, no hard feelings, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There is no hard feelings. I understand the reasoning, and the captain's word is law. I am excited to see the great things you accomplish as the quartermaster of our ship."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right, whatever's best for the ship.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Precisely, and for the captain."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, I feel the presence of Uk'otoa smiling on all of us and I know that our partnership here will be a beneficial one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can only agree."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, this has been fun. If you need anything, as your quartermaster, you just let me know.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Trust me. I have a few connections that owe me a favor or two here on the island. Anyway, I need a day to acquire what is necessary. Nevertheless--" At this point she looks up at somebody past you and goes, "We're having a private discussion." You turn and follow her eye line and you see a human female with curled blonde hair. The curls have fallen in the moisture and the rain from the outside, dressed in a thick, ragged, black coat, holding a cup, and she goes, "Aye, don't worry about me. Just didn't expect this shore to drag you in quite so soon." There's a moment of tension.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Into the water?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Beneath the water, my friend."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do cannon-folk typically go on those trips, or do cannon-folk typically stay behind with the ship?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Gunner master, Nott, gunner master.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Gunner master.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That is up to you. You are welcome to stay behind. However, if things go correctly, I don't think there will be any worry of drowning."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. How can you promise that?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As Avantika points it out, she shows the crew and says, "In two days travel, we will make our way outside of the Dragshallow Reef, coasting in northward for protection to about here. We begin looking for our salvage." At which point, Orly, who's glancing over the side, rubs his chin and goes, "Hmm. I don't mean to question the captain's m-m-main objective; however, that looks like it's right around where the Diver's Grave m-m-may be."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What purse?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The thing I imagine most adventurers have. A little coin satchel.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, but not like a cute little--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, not a Dolce & Gabbana, no. Not a Kate Spade.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You mean a competitor in terms of sea space, or in terms of your faith?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, we've crossed territories once or twice, and she has a habit of trying to embellish truths to the Plank King. Anyway." Hand me the map, if you don't mind. Oh, map's here, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I knew someone like that once. They would always go fishing and say that they caught something, but they never did.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: A fisherman?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Avantika lays a map out on the table.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, we'll put our weights down to weigh it down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, there was a discussion. It's coming.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah. Coasters are not heavy enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Here's a cube. Have a cube.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A cube will do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's a cube.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: There's a cube.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It is good, if you are going to do a night walk alone, or together as a group is much better than alone."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Not in particular. They'll be looking around here, they'll be preparing. Gather their materials, anything you think you require for the trip at sea go ahead and gather it, go amongst the Sundry Square, purchase what is required, and then hopefully within the next 48 hours we'll be back out on the waves."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 48 hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wonderful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What's Allison been up to?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Drinking in the back, having a conversation with somebody and keeping one eye over at the table. Seems just perturbed. Not directly intent, but occasionally narrowing her eyes in the direction of Avantika.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Being that this is our first time in Darktow, I would like to see the rest of the area. Would anyone care to join me for a stroll?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, night walk?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That sounds like a great idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: For the record, you guys do wait for about an hour and a half, two hours-- no sign of Allison.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sure. I'm so glad you put it that way. You're a good man, Fjord, so be careful, because I imagine it's a little out of character being honest and truthful with somebody in a deceptive way. It can muddle a good man up whereas it doesn't muddle a bad man up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Haven't been very good at it so far.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's one of the reasons why I like you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Here for you, man. I've been learning to swim.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, more of that to come. Where's the rest of these chuckle-fucks?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, two are mysteriously vanished. I think they were mumbling something about trying to find an alley--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Nott, will you stand guard now, because now I have to take a shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's been that long.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Travel from here to there and back, about four or five days round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If we're staying behind, I'd say give me about a hundred gold to watch over the nest around here. When you return, we'll hopefully-- I understand, perhaps, you m-m-might be coming back for us?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Without question.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Then I'll return whatever was not spent."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's a good idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've got 50 gold on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll get the other 50.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, kids. We are-- unless something goes tragically wrong-- we expect to be returning, and I imagine you'll hear word if things go tragically wrong.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Marius comes over and goes, "Right, now, hoping no tragedy, just come back and get us off this fucking hell-hole."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, if we don't come back, can't you take the Mistake and go home?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If you don't come back, I don't know."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: How long do we expect to be gone for?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. As you exit-- Caduceus, you notice this-- but Jester, as everyone's leaving, you to the back of it, you feel a hand grab your arm for a minute. You look over to the side, and you may not have noticed earlier, but Vera had been in the inn, in the tavern vicinity. She pulls you back from the rest of the group for a second and looks you in the eye with this intense stare. You can see the wrinkles on the sides of her face pushing into this tight crow's nest on each side, her irises bloodshot. She goes, "He would not be the first she's left in ruin, and he would not be the last. I will take care of him if he cannot." She releases you and spins around and walks off to her room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I figured we'll use the night air to our advantage, acclimate a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's take a walk. Let's see what there is to see. I'm excited about this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Learn the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yasha leans in and goes, "If there is any trouble, should I...?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, if there is trouble, let's try and resolve it-- well...

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I trust you, just--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Gauge it as it comes?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Tell me when you want--"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The Judge? Will do. It's so intimidating when she says it like that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I have taken, I'm assuming, a short rest and heal up from my fight while we were all chilling here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We should get a wig for the Judge like the Plank King has.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: For the sword?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A wig?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just throwing it out there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sort of like a golf club cover?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Like a tea-cozy. A sword-cozy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You guys leave the tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Up away from the docks.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. Both you and Beau make perception checks, please, since you're both keeping eyes out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How will they crack this if they hear us?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's all the code we need, all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How do we reference the--?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We call it the--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The Apricot.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The Apricot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The Apricot.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Anything else before we get into this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, is the Apricot this, or is the Apricot the tiny Tiffany Ship?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nope, it's this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's this, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Your heart? The Apricot is your heart?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Say that again?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's the tip of the tiny Tiffany Ship.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, so, how will we deal with Tiffany?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Which one's Tiffany?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's walk-and-talk about Tiffany.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Of course we're walking-and-talking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm keeping an eye open for every suspicious-looking asshole.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tiffany can be invisible though, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Everyone do this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Tiffany doesn't know that we're talking about her.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She went to bed, she's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to keep an eye out to see if the mist ever parts around us.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You do wander through what you assume to be the Sundry Square eventually, through this wandering. There is a makeshift pavilion where it's dotted with tables and crates under ratty awnings and canopies, but it's all closed and dark. There's no lights. It looks like none of the materials for sale are left behind. It's mainly just a structure and materials are probably brought there in the morning and are placed out for trade, sell, or whatever.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Everyone do this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Tiffany doesn't know that we're talking about her.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She went to bed, she's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to keep an eye out to see if the mist ever parts around us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Both you and Beau make perception checks, please, since you're both keeping eyes out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Come on!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She won't know we're talking about her because we're using a new name, Tiffany, so she's not even going to know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You're keeping a gauge on any of the wandering, patrolling guards to make sure as to not to speak too loudly or in the presence of anyone; conversation either shifting temporarily or coming to a lull and walking by in silence, but you manage to snake your way through a relatively quiet town beyond the various taverns this late at night.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A lot of store fronts and things that are closed up for the night.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You do know Darktow, before it was taken by pirates about 45 years or so beforehand, it was just another city of the Clovis Concord. It belonged to the Menagerie Coast, and it was one of the central port stops for a lot of the trade routes making their way to the coast. Then it was taken by the Revelry, and they reinforced it and made it defensible. They didn't slaughter everyone there, because they weren't there to kill everyone. They were there to take it over. They killed those who were, in their opinion, incorrectly placed in charge, any dissenters, and then anybody who agreed to join the Revelry, "Join or Die" type circumstance, everyone who had previously been at the island were absorbed into the Revelry. Some of them have continued their life there, some of them, their bloodline has continued to be those that maintain other forms of business. Not everyone is technically a pirate; there are a lot of members of the Revelry that are just civilians of Darktow and maintain the island.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Don't you think it's weird that someone would be a pirate and they would come here because they were a pirate and then they change their mind and decide to be a shopkeeper here instead?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No. Maybe they were looking for a new environment and they found exactly what they were looking for.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe they thought they were tough and strong, and they were not as tough and strong as they thought, so now they sell rope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a history check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It feels like a farmer's market. It feels like certain people sell things whenever they got them.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, natural 17, for 19.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: For all that you've been drinking over the past 24 hours, the tension has caused your bladder to retain everything and you're barely eking out a sputtering stream.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, it doesn't make any more sense, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I wander like a drunkard past Caleb and I go: Oh, so distracting. Like I need distractions.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if I make another hole in the floor and we keep going down until we can get out.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I guess she will have seen one hole by now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Apologies to my German teacher from high school. I start pissing on the docks and singing: Laurentia, liebe Laurentia mein.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are there any portal windows?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In the room you're in right now? No.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Am Sonntag. (speaking) Urination. (singing) Ach wenn es doch endlich schon--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to grab that jewel that was on the floor.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go ahead and make a performance check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Well, I'm definitely pissing and singing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you are.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yep. I have rolled such shit tonight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So sorry buddy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's a five. (singing) Von Dienstag.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It takes you awhile to do that. It's a few minutes to paint the wood properly to have the effect kick in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For all that you've been drinking over the past 24 hours, the tension has caused your bladder to retain everything and you're barely eking out a sputtering stream.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay. Well, I'll do my best to knock things over as I go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There isn't anything to knock over, these are open docks.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (shouting) Hey, Macarena!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I stand up on the bed and try to make the hole look like it wasn't perfectly painted, like it was like broken wood?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, wait! Just paint wood there!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I do that, though?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Can't paint on nothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I wonder if I can use one of my pieces of paper to like put on there and then make it wood, like, fix it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you do that and as soon as you do that the paper falls as a plank of wood.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's what I thought was going to happen, but I wanted to see.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, no, no.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Still going. It's taking her a few minutes. You're still doing your distraction. Travis, you're doing good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) We will, we will, rock you. Rock you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So, guy who was left, that guy is an asshole, and what's with that guy that just got stabbed? I feel very unsafe.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You feel unsafe in Darktow?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm just saying--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Welcome to Darktow."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He tried to, I think, rob me? I thought, as a pirate, that I had immunity to that? I don't know, I'm just trying--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He shifts in close and he looks at you real closely and goes, "Actually, I don't think I've seen you around here before."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Actually, technically, I'm new. Great to meet you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) B-A-N-A-N-A-S!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We're going to go ahead and have a conversation off the docks. Please follow me."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure! Let's go!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He pats you on the shoulder and starts leading you off the dock.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You're fucking welcome. Go, go, go, go, go!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How's her painting going?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A few moments pass. When you finish painting, the wood falls. While that's happening, that moment is occurring, you and Yasha, in the tavern, are keeping an eye. You watch as one of the crewmates comes walking in rather rapidly and goes and talks to somebody in the corner that you recognize from Avantika's crew.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You feel unsafe in Darktow?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm just saying--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Welcome to Darktow."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He tried to, I think, rob me? I thought, as a pirate, that I had immunity to that? I don't know, I'm just trying--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He shifts in close and he looks at you real closely and goes, "Actually, I don't think I've seen you around here before."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Actually, technically, I'm new. Great to meet you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) B-A-N-A-N-A-S!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We're going to go ahead and have a conversation off the docks. Please follow me."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure! Let's go!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He pats you on the shoulder and starts leading you off the dock.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You're fucking welcome. Go, go, go, go, go!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How's her painting going?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Still going. It's taking her a few minutes. You're still doing your distraction. Travis, you're doing good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Good show.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll open the door. Is there any ice? No? Okay.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It takes you a few minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I appreciate that. You're too kind, really." Zoen goes "See? No hard feelings, right?" and Waldock decks him across the chin. Zoen falls to the ground grabbing the side of his face.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Why don't you get that man a drink?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I think I'm going to do that." He gets up. While that's happening, and that's happening, and that's happening.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Hast du etwas Zeit fur mich?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm going to do what I originally planned which is to make the hole not look like a perfect hole. To make it look like the planks broke or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh wait, no, should I do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're going to find a hole.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Leave the fucking hole the way it is! We'll leave a couple gems in the bed.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Two gems.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Two gems in the bed.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: One gem. I'll break it in two.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a strength check.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You have to take your dagger and score it a bit and then try and press it again. Better part of three or four minutes to try to find the right edge to bend and break it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Why don't you get that man a drink?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I think I'm going to do that." He gets up. While that's happening, and that's happening, and that's happening.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Hast du etwas Zeit fur mich?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm going to do what I originally planned which is to make the hole not look like a perfect hole. To make it look like the planks broke or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh wait, no, should I do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're going to find a hole.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Leave the fucking hole the way it is! We'll leave a couple gems in the bed.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Two gems.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Two gems in the bed.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: One gem. I'll break it in two.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a strength check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It takes you a few minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, come on!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Correct. For one minute, so it's faded by now. As you guys are walking by, you hear the guards going like, "What's wrong?" He's like, "Ugh! I'm hurt!" He looks up and goes, "But it was on-- It was burning! What the fuck?!" He's confused and in pain and lamenting why he jumped and what's going on. The guards are trying to calm him a bit, and they're now starting to think something's up. You can see, now somebody is giving out directions to the other guards. Something is definitely not right, and the docks are now going into heightened search mode.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean, just calmly walking away, right? Are there lots more people coming?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They're coming toward the screaming and yelling. There are still others there that are patrolling and keeping an eye. They don't all of a sudden go: Someone's yelling! Everyone lose their post!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you think I seem noticeable?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, you look great.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're both wet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but we're Disguise Self, so we look dry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You look great. Do I look okay? Light my cigarette.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I feel like you may be less noticeable than a purple me.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You've got a cloak on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's pandemonium on the docks, right? At this point?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's borderline, but it's starting to calm a bit. Some of them have gotten up there, and they found the hurt guy. The spell lasts for how long?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Disguise Self? Or--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, the--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. The Phantasmal Force?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: To each of your doors, shortly after sunrise.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Where does that go?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: In the Bag of Holding.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I take out the silver thread first.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are we all staying in this room tonight?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No. I'm going to my room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it stupid to be literally down the hall decoding this journal all night from where Avantika's staying?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Too late.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, we've got the silver thread up. She can't get in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's just a warning system, right? It doesn't keep her out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It'll wake us up, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It'll wake us up. So who's guarding Caleb?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll guard Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So will Yasha.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so you guys will take sleeping shifts.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Mark yourself with one point of exhaustion, Caleb. You don't recover spells. The rest of you get a full rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I only used one.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, new spells.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I attack Fjord, sneak attack damage! Pretty good. (knocking)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What's that? Where is that? (knocking continues)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: By the way, all of your doors now are being held open and they're expectantly waiting. The crew doesn't trust you, and they haven't really since you've been here. They're not like, "Cool, guys, let's go!" There's very much a, "We're getting everybody in there and shit, there's going to be conversations."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Send it with the book.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The next minute goes by, and two more figures show up and they go, "We're going to the ship now, gather your things. There's no more waiting. Come on."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll do it on the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have to get dressed, that's all. That's the thing

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You get a sense from the vocality here that things are not right. Probably relating to events that transpired through the evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Gee, I wonder.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You could just pop out of the room and meet us on the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well yeah, they're not in the room. Do you want to pop out of the room?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Pop out of the room that--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, I mean Jester pops out of the room with the bag and leave it somewhere, then meet us on the way to the ship.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh that. The haversack?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Dimension door the fuck out of the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well what are we doing with the book and stuff?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Not now.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Sorry, in two weeks-- (screaming)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is this a frequent occurrence when you're in Darktow? This sort of--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, certainly not. To be perfectly honest, whoever did this does not understand the rules of the city very well."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I can-- No. I can find creatures. I can't find objects.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Hmm. Vera?" Vera's sitting back there and nods. You see her fingers trace this weird symbol in the air that leaves a slight trace. Her eyes flare for a second and she goes, "But that is okay. Because I can."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I take a bit of phosphorus and squelch it through my hand and cast Wall of Fire across the entire length of the boat between them and us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's where we're going to pick up next week. (screaming)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The gauntlet has been thrown!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sweating.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Next week we pick up in the shipyard of Darktow, on the deck--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not next week!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Oh my god, in two weeks! Oh, no! Curse you, Thanksgiving! On the deck of the Squalleater!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, certainly not. To be perfectly honest, whoever did this does not understand the rules of the city very well."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I can-- No. I can find creatures. I can't find objects.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Hmm. Vera?" Vera's sitting back there and nods. You see her fingers trace this weird symbol in the air that leaves a slight trace. Her eyes flare for a second and she goes, "But that is okay. Because I can."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I take a bit of phosphorus and squelch it through my hand and cast Wall of Fire across the entire length of the boat between them and us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's where we're going to pick up next week. (screaming)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The gauntlet has been thrown!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sweating.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Next week we pick up in the shipyard of Darktow, on the deck--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not next week!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, in two weeks-- (screaming)
